publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday April 22, 2018 at 21:08

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, writings, CDs, DVDs, photographs, personal papers, artwork, printed material, electronic media, and other papers by or relating to Charles North.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes correspondence to Rodney Phillips.

did.unittitle: Assorted - Publishing, Readings, and Other Events

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Charles North's journals, with drafts of poems and notes.

did.unittitle: Holabird, Jean - drawings by Tony Towle and Charles North at The Poetry Project at St. Mark's Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two of these drawings were used for the cover of Hanging Loose, no. 64.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains eight original pieces of artwork by Paula North: one pencil sketch of Charles North, The Poetry Project Newsletter, no. 127 (February / March 1988) cover layout, a photocopy of a painting of Charles North, and five watercolors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains one pencil sketch of Charles North, and one proof for Paul Violi's In Baltic Circles (1973).

did.unittitle: North, Paula - posters and reading flyers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two poetry reading flyers and five posters for events at The Poetry Project at St. Mark's Church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Charles North's birth annoucement (1941), passports for both Charles North and Paula North (1972), and Charles North's selective service card.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains approximately 60 black and white and color photographs, documenting aspects of Charles North's life.

did.unittitle: Poetry - lists of manuscripts sent out
Poetry - lists of published poetry and readings
Broadway: A Poets and Painters Anthology
Broadway (Swollen Magpie Press) - correspondence, submission letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typescript sent by James Schuyler and Charles North, as well as submission letters from: Keith Abbott, John Ashbery, Bill Berkson, Ted berrigan, Jim Brodey, Michael Brownstein, Reed Bye, Douglas Crase, Robert Dash, Kenward Elmslie, Larry Fagin, Mary Ferrari, John Godfrey, Barbara Guest, Steve Hamilton, Yuki Hartman, Ralph Hawkins, Alex Katz, Vincent Katz, Kenneth Koch, John Koethe, Michael Lally, Steve Levine, Bernadette Mayer, Eileen Myles, Maureen Owen, Pat Nolan, Ron Padgett, Frank Polach, Anne Porter, Anne Waldman, Lewis Warsh, and Trevor Winkfield.

did.unittitle: Broadway (Swollen Magpie Press) - correspondence, received after publication

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from: Keith Abbott, Robert T. Bath, Bill Berkson, Kenward Elmslie, Alex Katz, Pat Nolan, James Sherry, Lewis Warsh, and Trevor Winkfield.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the original manuscripts as submitted by the poets. Keith Abbott's poem is not present.

did.unittitle: Broadway (Swollen Magpie Press) - cover, original artwork by Paula North
Broadway 2 (Hanging Loose Press) - correspondence, James Schuyler and Charles North

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence asking for submissions, as well as a list of prospective contributors for Broadway 2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes submission letters from: Mary Abbott, John Ash, John Ashbery, Bill Berkson, Charles Bernstein, Nell Blaine, Joe Brainard, Donna Brook, Rudy Burckhardt, Reed Bye, Rosemary Ceravolo (Joe), Maxine Chernoff, Elaine Equi, Larry Fagin, Mary Ferrari, Ed Friedman, Peter Gizzi, John Godfrey, Yuki Hartman, Gerrit Henry, Robert Hershon, Paul Hoover, Vincent Katz, John Koethe, Art Lange, Ann Lauterbach, Gary Lenhart, Bernadette Mayer, Alice Notley, Maureen Owen, Ron Padgett, Simon Pettet, Anne Porter, J.H. Prynne, Eugene Richie, Jerome Sala, Michael Scholnick, David Shapiro, Ron Silliman, Paul Violi, Anne Waldman, Lewis Warsh, Rosanne Wasserman, Trevor Winkfield, and Geoffrey Young.

did.unittitle: Broadway 2 (Hanging Loose Press) - correspondence, received after publication

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from: Rudy Burckhardt, Kenward Elmslie, Vincent Katz, Anne David Lehman, Simon Pettet, Lewis Warsh, and Geoff Young.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the original manuscripts as submitted by the poets.

did.unittitle: Broadway 2 (Hanging Loose Press) - manuscripts not published
Broadway 2 (Hanging Loose Press) - assorted production and business
"Interview with Sarah Fox", typescript and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The interview was published in Verse, vol. 20, nos. 2/3 (2004).

did.unittitle: Leap Year, original artwork by Paula North

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains 4 drawings and title page artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains 6 original drawings.

did.unittitle: Complete Lineups (Hanging Loose Press), original cover artwork by Paula North

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the sketch and complete cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Lineup I" and "Lineup II", with assorted other manuscripts.

did.unittitle: Elizabethan and Nova Scotian Music (Adventures in Poetry)
The Nearness of the Way you Look Tonight (Adventures in Poetry)
The Nearness of the Way You Look Tonight (Adventures in Poetry)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains bound proof, with rejected cover.

did.unittitle: New and Selected Poems (Sun Moon Press)
No Other Way: Selected Prose (Hanging Loose Press)
No Other Way: Selected Prose (Hanging Loose Press), bound proof
"No Other Way: Selected Prose (Hanging Loose Press) - Assorted"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a mock-up for an unpublished chapbook, with cover and drawing by Trevor Winkfield.

did.unittitle: Ode to Asparagus, Peonies and Manet (The Song Cave)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a typescript with corrections and notes. For a talk at the Pace Active Retirement Center (PARC), 1988 November 15.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains five original graphite drawings, by Paula North.

did.unittitle: What It Is Like: New and Selected Poems (Turtle Point Press / Hanging Loose)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the initial manuscript.

did.unittitle: What It Is Like: New and Selected Poems (Turtle Point Press / Hanging Loose)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains assorted proof pages with corrections.

did.unittitle: What It Is Like: New and Selected Poems (Turtle Point Press / Hanging Loose), unbound proof
Year of the Olive (Hanging Loose Press), uncorrected page proof

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains pages torn from journals.

did.unittitle: Tulips (poems with drawings by Trevor Winkfield; Phylum)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscript material, notes and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material written by Charles North for other people.

did.unittitle: Introductions by Charles North for poetry readings and other events
Book covers for Charles North books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains cover proofs for New and Selected Poems (1999), The Nearness of the Way You Look Tonight (2001), No Other Way: Selected Prose (1998), and Cadenza (2007).

did.unittitle: The Franklin Library. The Great Gatsby [by] F. Scott Fitzgerald.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes from the Editors series, from The Franklin Mint Library. Ghost written by Charles North.

did.unittitle: The Franklin Library. The Scarlet Letter by Nathanial Hawthorne.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes from the Editors series, from The Franklin Mint Library. Ghost written by Charles North.

did.unittitle: The Franklin Library. Tristram Shandy by Laurence Stern.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes from the Editors series, from The Franklin Mint Library. Ghost written by Charles North.

did.unittitle: North, Charles and Paula North. A Pressed Wafer, postcards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes three postcards: "All-Stars II: p Cobb," "Plotinus c," and "Mozart cf."

did.unittitle: North, Charles and Paula North. Clip from Francis Jammes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four variant broadsides of artwork, by Paula North. Illustrated with colored pencil and paint.

did.unittitle: North, Charles and Paula North. Port of Authority

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original artwork, signed by Charles North and Paula North. Published in The Poetry Project Newsletter, No. 91 (1992 May).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed to Charles North.

did.unittitle: Ceravolo, Joseph - The Green Lake is Awake (Coffee House Press)
Ceravolo, Joseph - The Green Lake is Awake, selection lists, tables of contents, etc.
Ceravolo, Joseph - The Green Lake is Awake, materials for selection
Ceravolo, Joseph - The Green Lake is Awake, publicity
Crase, Doug - "A Voice Like the Day"
Guest, Barbara - "Le Mesa Norte / for Paula North"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed by Barbara Guest.

did.unittitle: Hershon, Robert - "Bushwick and Menahan: The Center of All Beauty / For Charles North"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed to Charles North.

did.unittitle: Koethe, John - assorted prose and poetry
Schuyler, James - The Morning of the Poem (Farrar, Straus and Giroux)
Shapiro, David - A Burning Interior (Overlook Press)
Towle, Tony - The History of the Invitation: New and Selected Poems 1963-2000 (Hanging Loose Press)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains assorted poems.

did.unittitle: Towle, Tony - New and Selected Poems (1963-1983) (Kulcher Foundation)
Violi, Paul - "A List For Charles North"
Violi, Paul - The Tame Magpie (Hanging Loose Press)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the "final manuscript", with notes on the top page. Also contains rejects and notes from Charles North and Tony Towle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes material which was published as Fracas (Hanging Loose Press, 1999).

did.unittitle: Violi, Paul and Scott Gendel - "Violi Songs" with Scott Gendel lectures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains collaborative annotations critical of poems within the New Yorker magazine.

did.unittitle: Waldman, Anne - "5 Ghost Days / for Charles North"
Adler, Ellen, Seaver Leslie, Paula North, Darragh Park, and Jane Ritchie. Five Realists. (Hirschl Adler Galleries)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a copy of the original Yale University Press edition of "Some Trees," by John Ashbery (1956). This copy was made for Charles North by Frances [Weldman?].

did.unittitle: Baxter, Glen. "Plus Four Soup." (The Alternative Press) - postcard
Berrigan, Sandy - postcard removed from Daily Rites (Telephone Books, 1974)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a postcard from Sandy Berrigan to Paula North, who was the artist for the book.

did.unittitle: Brainard, Joe Kenward Elmslie. "Sex in Suburbia." (The Alternative Press) - postcard
Chen, Ada - correspondence removed from Spilled Milk (self-published on Blurb)
Dorfman, Elsa. "Gordon Cairnie and John Weiners at the Grolier Bookshop." (Pressed Wafer) - postcard
Elmslie, Kenward. "Champ Dust Spinoff no. 10: Lost Boy Wrap." (The Alternative Press) - postcard
Elmslie, Kenward and Ken Tisa. "Palais Bimbo Snapshots." (The Alternative Press) - postcard
Ginsberg, Allen. "In the Benjo." (The Alternative Press) - postcard
Hershon, Robert. "Incredible Shrinking Menu." (The Alternative Press) - postcard
Hershon, Robert. "Wambsganns and Grimes." (The Alternative Press) - postcard
Kyger, Joanne and Donald Guravitch. "Original Postcard." (The Alternative Press) - hand-colored postcard
Lillywhite, Harvey. Catalogue of Audio (and Video) Tapes: Readings, Performance, Workshops and Lectures at the St. Mark's Poetry Project, 1970-1979. (The Poetry Project)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recordings by Charles North are listed.

did.unittitle: Malanga, Gerard - Post-it note removed from Cristinas World Im(media)cy Poemworks (Poetry on Films)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Post-it note from Charles North, previously attached to the poem "the poet charles north beats me to the punch", on page 49 of Cristinas World Im(media)cy Poemworks.

did.unittitle: Monday. Spring 1975 - Post-it note removed from magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A one-shot magazine published in conjunction with the Charles North workshop, taught at The Poetry Project.

did.unittitle: Padgett, Ron. "Four Caribbean Songs." (The Alternative Press) - postcard
Rand, Archie and John Yau. "A Modest man never stalks himself." (The Alternative Press) - postcard
Stannard, Martin - material removed from Conversations with Myself: Selected Reviews and Notes 1984-1998
Towle, Tony. Poetic Income: A Report [self published]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains No. 9 from an edition of 500. Inscribed by Tony Towle to Charles and Paula North.

did.unittitle: Towle, Tony and Rachel Towle. "The Interesting Story" [self published]
Violi, Paul. "Buckeroo." (Stone Press Weekly no. 74) - postcard
Wagner, Richard, Paul Sérusier, and Trevor Winkfield. Reminder - booklet
Waldman, Anne. "Writing Rapprochment - 'The Colonies'." (The Alternative Press) - postcard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains postcard with original collage elements.

did.unittitle: Winkfield, Trevor. "Trevor Winkfield." (Tibor de Nagy Gallery) - catalog
